title: KDB Compiled Source Response (per-call model output)
description: Exactly one source -> exactly one response object. The LLM emits this shape per compile call. Source-id-space fields (top-level source_id, per-page supports_page_existence, per-log_entry related_source_ids) are NOT in this contract — the runner injects them after parse from runtime knowledge + manifest lookup. This separation is structural: the LLM owns slug-space, the runner owns source_id-space. Run-level fields (run_id, success, aggregate errors) are also Python-owned; see compile_result.schema.json. This contract is stricter than compile_result.schema.json's pageIntent (all 7 LLM-emitted page fields required) so the model must commit to status, links, and confidence on every page rather than relying on Python backfill.

slug.description: Lowercase ASCII kebab-case identifier. No paths, no .md suffixes, no directory separators. The 'summary-' prefix is reserved (see summarySlug).

summarySlug.description: Slug for the per-source summary page. MUST start with the reserved 'summary-' prefix followed by the kebab-cased source filename stem. Concept and article slugs do NOT use this prefix. Reserving the prefix structurally prevents within-source slug collision between the summary and a concept whose name matches the source's main topic.

sourceName.description: The source filename the LLM was given, echoed verbatim. No path separators — the runner constructs the full source_id from runtime knowledge after parse.

pageIntent.description: One page the LLM wants to create or replace (full-body model, D18). Stricter than compile_result.schema.json: all 7 LLM-emitted fields required. Source-id-space field supports_page_existence is runner-injected post-parse.

body.description: Full markdown body, no frontmatter. Python prepends frontmatter during apply.

outgoing_links.description: Slugs this page's body links to. Must appear in body as [[slug]]. Python reconciles incoming_links_known from these.

logEntry.description: One structured note about this compile. Source-id-space field related_source_ids is runner-derived post-parse from related_slugs via manifest lookup.
